title: geonoma undata subsp. tacarcunensis
intro: Geonoma undata subsp. tacarcunensis, also known as Tacarcun palm, is a subspecies of palm tree native to the tropical regions of Central America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Geonoma undata subsp. tacarcunensis include treating various ailments such as fever, skin infections, and respiratory issues, as well as being used as an anti-parasitic agent. In terms of herbal preparations, the plant can be used in various forms, including infusions, decoctions, and topical applications, often combined with other herbs to enhance its effects.
